title: rumohra walkerae
intro: Rumohra walkerae, also known as the Maidenhair fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polypodiaceae. It is traditionally used in various herbal medicine practices, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in digestion, as well as helping to manage diabetes and hypertension. Rumohra walkerae can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are often used in traditional medicine practices.
